title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR HARISSA (Red hot pepper paste) (Neer East) 1 CXS 308R-2011

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR HARISSA (Red hot pepper paste) (Neer East) 1 CXS 308R-2011
sections.summary: Title and Codex identifier: Regional Standard for Harissa (red hot pepper paste), CXS 308R-2011 (document heading).

sections.section: Adopted in 2011. Subject to endorsement by CCFL, CCFA and CCMAS.
sections.summary: Administrative note stating the standard was adopted in 2011 and is subject to endorsement by CCFL, CCFA and CCMAS, with a footnote pointing to the Codex website for Near East member lists.

Applies to harissa (red hot pepper paste) as defined in Section 2 that is manufactured and intended for direct human consumption, and explicitly excludes other traditionally-prepared types of harissa.
Defines 'harissa' as the preserved, concentrated pulp of fresh red hot pepper (Capsicum annuum) with the composition specified in Section 3 and preserved by thermal treatment only.

Lists mandatory ingredients (fresh Capsicum annuum peppers, fresh garlic, coriander, caraway and salt) and specifies quality factors for raw peppers and the final product, including sensory and texture requirements and numeric limits: total acidity ≤3.6% (as citric acid of total dry residues), dry extract ≥14% of total soluble solids (excluding salt), added salt ≤1.5% (dry weight), and acid-insoluble ash ≤0.15% of dry extract.
Lists the ingredients permitted in processed harissa: fresh red hot peppers (Capsicum annuum), fresh garlic, coriander, caraway and salt.

Requires that peppers used be ripe, wholesome and free of spoilage, and sets final-product quality criteria covering sensory (uniform red color, typical spicy taste and smell), texture (no crusts or seeds when sieved at 2 mm) and compositional limits for acidity, dry extract, added salt and acid-insoluble ash (see Section 3.2.2 numeric limits).
Requires raw peppers for processed harissa to be sufficiently ripe, wholesome, deep red in colour and free from spoilage, rot, impurities and insects.

Specifies final-product requirements: homogeneous natural red colour (hunter a/b value ≥1/4) free of black particles, typical spicy taste and smell, free of crusts and seeds when using a 2 mm sieve, total acidity ≤3.6% (as citric acid of total dry residues), dry extract ≥14% of total soluble solids excluding salt, added salt ≤1.5% (dry weight), and acid-insoluble ash ≤0.15% of dry extract.

Requires a natural homogeneous red colour (hunter a/b value not less than 1/4) free of black particles, a typical spicy (hot) taste free from bitterness or burned/foreign tastes, and a typical smell free of foreign odours.
States the final product must be free of crusts and seeds when tested using a 2 mm sieve.
Limits total acidity (expressed as citric acid) to not more than 3.6% of total dry residues.
Requires dry extract to be at least 14% of total soluble solids (excluding salt).
Limits the percentage of added salt to not exceed 1.5% on a dry weight basis.
Specifies a compositional limit for harissa: acid‑insoluble ash must not exceed 0.15% of the total weight of the dry extract.
States a prohibition: no food additives may be used in harissa.
Requires compliance for the product with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CXS 193‑1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends that harissa be prepared and handled following the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969) and other relevant Codex texts, and that it comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21‑1997.

Requires minimum fill of the container: the product should occupy not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (allowing necessary head space per GMP), defines water capacity as volume of distilled water at 20°C the sealed container holds, and states flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable.
Heading only: '7.1 Fill of Container' with no substantive provisions in this entry.

Specifies minimum fill requirements for containers: product must occupy at least 90% of the container's water capacity (minus necessary head space per GMP) and flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable, with water capacity defined at 20°C.
Requires labelling in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985) and specifically mandates the product name be 'harissa'.
Requires harissa to be packed in suitable, clean, tightly closed containers using only packaging materials authorized for food contact.
Not substantive: methods of analysis and sampling are indicated as 'To be completed.'
